name: rasa.orchestrator.canvas
description: The vertical-builder orchestrator: authors and maintains canvas-vertical UIs (rasa.layout.v1 documents + sandboxed html artifacts) conversationally. Each app it manages is a session keyed by its own workspace directory; the orchestrator bootstraps that directory, owns the app's canvas, handles ui_event interactions as the app's runtime brain, and keeps the screens honest to the component contract in content/.

rasa.tenant_model: The element definition is tenant-agnostic (the species); its INSTANCES are tenant-resident: sessions run with cwd inside a tenant's tree, so the tenant is the parent context (its CLAUDE.md/files/data in scope, kernel stamps tenant identity) and every app it manages is one of that tenant's UI surfaces. Composition target: tenant (where) x domain.* (about) x orchestrator.canvas (how).
rasa.session_model: one app = one session, keyed (element=rasa.orchestrator.canvas, cwd=<tenant>/.rasaos/apps/<app-id>). The app directory is bootstrapped by this orchestrator on first contact and doubles as the app's persistent store, inside the tenant's own tree.
rasa.canvas_model: one canvas per app session (kernel keys canvases by session until args.canvas_id lands — see kernel asks in content/KERNEL_ASKS.md).
rasa.note: The renderer contract lives with the shell (frontend-rasaos, SA-026/027/028); this element carries the AUTHORING doctrine. Keep the two in lockstep via content/COMPONENTS.md.

scaffold.description: No install scaffold — this orchestrator operates in-session.
